marketState: neutral-to-slightly bearish - rich vs fair value and a storage surplus outweigh hot-weather demand support
summary: NG front trades at $3.293, about $0.36 above the 'now' fair-value estimate of $2.929 (upper half of the $2.43-$3.43 band), and the premium narrows at longer horizons as fair value itself drifts higher (to $3.07 by +21D) - a path that argues for reversion rather than further chase. Weather demand is running well above the 5-year normal in both the 1-7D (+16%, rangePos 0.96) and 8-16D (+20.6%, rangePos 0.84) windows and still rising, the main bullish input, but with 1-7D demand already near the top of its own seasonal range there is limited room for further upside surprise. Seasonality is modestly supportive (price +4.7pp above the typical-year path, 63% historical odds of being higher in 4 weeks) but reliability is explicitly flagged Low this week (51pp spread). Storage is running a +175 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average with a bearish, bigger-than-forecast weekly build, and daily/weekly technicals show a 50<200 EMA death-cross with a bearish weekly trend read, both working against the bull case. Net: the setup favors fading current richness back toward fair value over chasing the seasonal/weather tailwind, with low-to-medium conviction given the genuinely mixed inputs.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; consider half-size given Low seasonality reliability
longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus of +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise, price sitting above fair value at every horizon (now through +21D), and a 50<200 EMA death-cross on both daily and weekly timeframes argue for fading the current premium, even though seasonality (63% odds higher at 4wks) and weather trend are modestly supportive.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily/weekly close above $3.57 (the +21D fair-value band top) or a bullish storage surprise (draw bigger than forecast) confirming a supply deficit would invalidate this reversion-lower thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is 'SLIGHTLY OVER' fair value on the near-dated band with only ~$0.14 of room left to the $3.43 band top, 1-7D weather demand is already near the top of its own range (rangePos 0.96, little room for further upside surprise), and daily technicals show a bearish EMA cross despite bullish 1h/4h momentum - favors a mean-reversion short toward the fair-value zone.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close above $3.43-3.45 or a fresh spike in 1-7D weather demand beyond its current near-max range would invalidate the short and favor a momentum long instead.
